memoryArtifacts.headline: Margaret, wife of Richard Cater/Carter III and his second wife, Elizabeth.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Margaret, wife of Richard Cater/Carter III and his second wife, Elizabeth.
by Robert Givens

Almost nothing is known about her and her life.  There are 4 children attributed to her.  These children …

memoryArtifacts.headline: The 3 Richard Caters found in Genealogical Dictionary of Maine and New Hampshire Vo. 2 p. 133
Richard Cater III Land Grant of 1694 for Town of Dover
memoryArtifacts.description:
ew Hampshire, Town Clerk Vital and Town Records, 1636-1947
Dover Town Records, Vol. 3, p. 20) Image 14 of 253

On 11 April 1694 Richard III was granted land adjoining his father's plantation:
 	Aprill 11th 1694
By ye Comitte Chosen By ye ffreeholders of ye Towne of Dover for granting Lands given and granted unto Richard Cater his heires and assignes for Ever twenty Acors of Land as an addition to his ffathers Plantation Test will ffurber Clark of sd Comitte The Above grant voated & Confermed in generalll Towne metting The sixteenth day of Aprill 1694    Test Jno Tuttle  Towne Clerk
(Dover Town Records, Vol. 3, p. 20)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Carter III於1673年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯時，他的父親Richard Cater II34歲，母親Mary Richard22歲。他於大約1693年與Elizabeth在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1713年6月13日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Carter III于1673年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯时，他的父亲Richard Cater II34岁，母亲Mary Richard22岁。他于大约1693年与Elizabeth在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1713年6月13日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Carter III was born in 1673, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, his father, Richard Cater II, was 34 and his mother, Mary Richard, was 22. He married Elizabeth about 1693, in New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 13 June 1713, in Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 40.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Carter IIIが1673年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスで生まれた時，父Richard Cater IIは34歳，母Mary Richardは22歳であった。約1693年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアでElizabethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1713年6月13日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムニューイントンで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Carter III(이)가 1673년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Cater II(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Richard(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1693년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 Elizabeth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1713년 6월 13일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 뉴잉턴에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Carter III nasceu em 1673, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, seu pai, Richard Cater II, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mary Richard, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth aproximadamente 1693, em New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de junho de 1713, em Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 40 anos.

ru.North Church of Portsmouth, Portsmouth,: норт чурч оф порцмоут, порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Carter III родился в 1673 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Richard Cater II было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Richard, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth около 1693 г. в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 13 июня 1713 г. в местности нюингтон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Carter III nació en 1673, en Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Cater II, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mary Richard, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Elizabeth aproximadamente 1693, en New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de junio de 1713, en Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 40 años.
